sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, description STRING, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'hello world; 42', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (2, NULL, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02 11:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (3, 'test string; 100', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-03 12:00:00');
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (5, 'boundary case; 9', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-05 14:00:00');
SELECT id, string_split_regex(description, '; ') AS split_desc FROM test_table WHERE created_at - TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01' > INTERVAL '0 days';
SELECT id, description, created_at, (string_split_regex(description, '; ')[1]::INTEGER - 42) AS difference FROM test_table WHERE description IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, string_split_regex(description, '; ') AS split_desc FROM test_table WHERE string_split_regex(description, '; ')[1] IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY created_at ORDER BY id) AS count_per_date FROM test_table;
SELECT t1.id, string_split_regex(t2.description, ';? ') AS split_desc FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.created_at - TIMESTAMP '2022-12-31' = INTERVAL '1 day';

WITH subquery AS (SELECT id, created_at, string_split_regex(description, '; ') AS split_desc FROM test_table WHERE created_at - TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01' < INTERVAL '5 days') SELECT id, split_desc FROM subquery WHERE split_desc[1]::INTEGER IS NOT NULL;
